  "instructions": [
    "Do not use sorry, admit, placeholders, ellipses, or comments standing in for proof.",

    "Do not inspect, infer, ask for, or name hidden aligned Lean target declarations.",
    "Use only the source text, visible prompt context, verifier-visible support context, and checked Mathlib/project/local facts shown in each task.",
    "Treat selector sketches and previous failed generations as diagnostic intent only; Lean-checked signatures and visible source text are authoritative.",
    "You may introduce same-unit helper definitions or lemmas before the main declaration when they are justified by visible source/context.",
    "Do not use a helper name as an existing fact until you have defined/proved it earlier in the same lean_file_body.",
    "Keep theorem statements source-shaped. Do not replace elementary source hypotheses by stronger library predicates unless you also construct those predicates locally in the proof.",
    "If a checked bridge lemma rewrites between indexed forms, use an explicit rw/change/calc route in the direction of the goal.",
    "For finite sums and products over a Finset, use Lean membership-binder notation `sum x in s` is invalid; write Lean syntax as `∑ x ∈ s, f x` or `∏ x ∈ s, f x`.",
    "Do not enlarge a finite source choice space to all functions into an infinite codomain. Preserve finite representations supplied by visible context.",
    "Every identifier in a generated declaration must be introduced by an explicit binder, local declaration, visible helper, checked Mathlib/project/local declaration, or import/open context visible to the verifier.",
    "If verifier messages report missing typeclass instances for an unbound symbol, such as `Zero K`, `CommRing K`, or `Fintype α`, add the required type variable and typeclass binders to the generated theorem or emit the corresponding visible `variable` command in lean_file_body. Do not rely on local_file_context_candidates variables unless you reproduce the needed variable command or binders in the output.",
    "If a task contains decline_context_pack.selected_project_context, treat those snippets as newly acquired visible project/local context after target-hidden filtering. Try those project declarations before concluding that a named project definition or bridge is unavailable.",
    "Do not describe a declaration as missing if its name appears in decline_context_pack.selected_project_context. If you still cannot use that declaration, notes must state the concrete reason: unresolved dependency, namespace/import mismatch, type/signature mismatch, or proof-synthesis gap.",
    "If the visible context is insufficient, set status to cannot_prove_from_visible_context, lean_file_body to exactly empty string, and declaration_names to exactly []. Put the missing facts in notes only."
  ],

    {

        ],

        "unit_key": "unit-001",
        "used_context": [

      "decline_context_pack": {
        "policy": "Additional project context acquired from clean proof-lane decline notes after same-source target-declaration exclusion filtering. Use these snippets as visible project context; do not infer hidden aligned target declarations.",
        "selected_project_context": [
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "catalanHankelMatrix",
            "kind": "def",
            "lean_snippet": "def catalanHankelMatrix (k : ℕ) : Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ℤ :=\n  Matrix.of fun i j => (catalan ((i : ℕ) + (j : ℕ)) : ℤ)\n\n/-- The Catalan Hankel matrix is symmetric (since c_{i+j} = c_{j+i}). -/",
            "line": 5147,
            "match_kind": "exact_local_name",
            "name": "LGV.catalanHankelMatrix",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "pathWeightMatrix",
            "kind": "def",
            "lean_snippet": "noncomputable def pathWeightMatrix {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] {D : SimpleDigraph V}\n    (hpf : D.IsPathFinite) (w : ArcWeight D K) {k : ℕ}\n    (A B : kVertex V k) : Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) K :=\n  Matrix.of fun i j => pathWeightSum hpf w (A i) (B j)\n\n/-!\n## LGV Lemma: Weighted Lattice Version\n\nTheorem thm.lgv.kpaths.wt: For the integer lattice with arc weights w,\ndet(M) = ∑_{σ ∈ Sₖ} (-1)^σ ∑_{𝐩 nipat from 𝐀 to σ(𝐁)} w(𝐩)\n-/\n\n/-- The set of non-intersecting path tuples from 𝐀 to 𝐁 -/",
            "line": 758,
            "match_kind": "exact_local_name",
            "name": "LGV.pathWeightMatrix",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "dyckDigraph_pathFinite",
            "kind": "theorem",
            "lean_snippet": "theorem dyckDigraph_pathFinite : dyckDigraph.IsPathFinite := by\n  intro u v\n  by_cases h : v.1 < u.1\n  · -- Case 1: No paths exist (would need to go backwards in x)\n    convert Set.finite_empty\n    ext p\n    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.mem_empty_iff_false, iff_false, not_and]\n    intro hstart hfinish\n    have hlen := dyckDigraph_path_length_eq p\n    rw [hstart, hfinish] at hlen\n    have hpos : 0 < p.vertices.length := List.length_pos_of_ne_nil p.nonempty\n    omega\n  · -- Case 2: Paths exist but are finite\n    push_neg at h\n    -- Define the path length\n    let n := (v.1 - u.1 + 1).toNat\n    -- Define the bounding box for the second coordinate\n    -- The second coordinate stays between 0 and u.2 + n\n    let maxY := u.2 + n\n    let box : Finset (ℤ × ℕ) := (Finset.Icc u.1 v.1) ×ˢ (Finset.range (maxY + 1))\n    -- Define the subtype of paths from u to v\n    let S := {p : SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph | p.start = u ∧ p.finish = v}\n    -- Show S is finite by injecting into Fin n → box\n    suffices Finite S by exact Set.finite_coe_iff.mpr this\n    -- Define the injection: map each path to its vertex list as a function\n    let f : S → (Fin n → box) := fun ⟨p, hp⟩ i =>\n      let hi : i.val < p.vertices.length := by\n        have hlen := dyckDigraph_path_length_eq p\n        rw [hp.1, hp.2] at hlen\n        simp only [n] at hlen ⊢\n        have := i.isLt\n        omega",
            "line": 5305,
            "match_kind": "exact_local_name",
            "name": "LGV.dyckDigraph_pathFinite",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "dyckUnitArcWeight",
            "kind": "def",
            "lean_snippet": "noncomputable def dyckUnitArcWeight : ArcWeight dyckDigraph ℤ :=\n  fun _ _ _ => 1\n\n/-- Helper: path weight with unit weight is 1 -/",
            "line": 5426,
            "match_kind": "exact_local_name",
            "name": "LGV.dyckUnitArcWeight",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "xDecreasing",
            "kind": "def",
            "lean_snippet": "def xDecreasing {k : ℕ} (A : kVertex (ℤ × ℤ) k) : Prop :=\n  ∀ i j : Fin k, i ≤ j → xCoord (A j) ≤ xCoord (A i)\n\n/-- A k-vertex has weakly increasing y-coordinates -/",
            "line": 3858,
            "match_kind": "exact_local_name",
            "name": "LGV.xDecreasing",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "yIncreasing",
            "kind": "def",
            "lean_snippet": "def yIncreasing {k : ℕ} (A : kVertex (ℤ × ℤ) k) : Prop :=\n  ∀ i j : Fin k, i ≤ j → yCoord (A i) ≤ yCoord (A j)\n\n/-- Discrete intermediate value theorem for integers (auxiliary version with explicit n) -/",
            "line": 3862,
            "match_kind": "exact_local_name",
            "name": "LGV.yIncreasing",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "nipatWeightSum",
            "kind": "def",
            "lean_snippet": "noncomputable def nipatWeightSum {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] {D : SimpleDigraph V}\n    (hpf : D.IsPathFinite) (w : ArcWeight D K) {k : ℕ}\n    (A B : kVertex V k) (_σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin k)) : K :=\n  ∑ pt ∈ nipatFinset hpf A B, pathTupleWeight w pt.paths\n\n/-!\n## Path Tuple Infrastructure for LGV\n\nThe key step in the LGV proof is to express the product of sums as a sum over path tuples:\n  ∏_j (∑_{p : A_j → B_j} w(p)) = ∑_{pt : PathTuple} pathTupleWeight w pt.paths\n\nThis uses `Finset.prod_univ_sum` and a bijection between PathTuple and the piFinset.\n-/\n\n/-- Bijection between PathTuple and the piFinset of pathsFromTo.\n    This is the key to converting the product of sums to a sum over path tuples. -/",
            "line": 818,
            "match_kind": "exact_local_name",
            "name": "LGV.nipatWeightSum",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "SimpleDigraph.Path",
            "kind": "structure",
            "lean_snippet": "structure Path (D : SimpleDigraph V) where\n  /-- The vertices of the path, in order -/\n  vertices : List V\n  /-- The path is nonempty -/\n  nonempty : vertices ≠ []\n  /-- Consecutive vertices are connected by arcs -/\n  arcs_valid : ∀ i : ℕ, ∀ hi : i + 1 < vertices.length, D.arc (vertices.get ⟨i, Nat.lt_of_succ_lt hi⟩)\n      (vertices.get ⟨i + 1, hi⟩)\n\n/-- The starting vertex of a path -/",
            "line": 97,
            "match_kind": "suffix_full_name",
            "name": "LGV.SimpleDigraph.Path",

            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          }

        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV2.tex",
        "referenced_labels": [],
        "source_text": "\\begin{corollary}\n\\label{cor.lgv.catalan-hankel-det-0}Let $k\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Recall the Catalan\nnumbers $c_{n}=\\dfrac{1}{n+1}\\dbinom{2n}{n}$ for all $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Then,%\n\\[\n\\det\\left(  \\left(  c_{i+j-2}\\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq k,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq k}\\right)\n=\\det\\left(\n\\begin{array}\n[c]{cccc}%\nc_{0} & c_{1} & \\cdots & c_{k-1}\\\\\nc_{1} & c_{2} & \\cdots & c_{k}\\\\\n\\vdots & \\vdots & \\ddots & \\vdots\\\\\nc_{k-1} & c_{k} & \\cdots & c_{2k-2}%\n\\end{array}\n\\right)  =1.\n\\]\n\n\\end{corollary}"
      },

                    "lean_snippet": "theorem lgv_nonpermutable {k : ℕ} (w : ArcWeight integerLattice K)\n    (A B : kVertex (ℤ × ℤ) k)\n    (hxA : xDecreasing A) (hyA : yIncreasing A)\n    (hxB : xDecreasing B) (hyB : yIncreasing B) :\n    (pathWeightMatrix integerLattice_pathFinite w A B).det =\n      nipatWeightSum integerLattice_pathFinite w A B (Equiv.refl (Fin k))",
                    "line_range": [
                      4264,
                      4311
                    ],
                    "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Determinants.LGV2",
                    "name": "LGV.lgv_nonpermutable",
                    "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/Determinants/LGV2.lean",
                    "referenced_source_labels": [],
                    "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",

                  },
                  {
                    "context_source": "referencing_prior_declaration",
                    "declared_source_labels": [
                      "thm.sf.jt-h"
                    ],
                    "imports": [
                      "Mathlib",
                      "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Determinants.LGV2",
                      "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.OmegaInvolution",
                      "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.NPartition",
                      "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.LittlewoodRichardson"
                    ],
                    "kind": "theorem",
                    "lean_snippet": "/-- First Jacobi-Trudi formula (Theorem thm.sf.jt-h)\n    Label: thm.sf.jt-h\n\n    s_{λ/μ} = det((h_{λᵢ - μⱼ - i + j})_{1 ≤ i,j ≤ M})\n\n    The proof uses the Lindström-Gessel-Viennot lemma with appropriate\n    source and target vertices in the integer lattice ℤ².\n\n    ## Proof Strategy\n\n    **Step 1: Set up the LGV framework**\n    Define source vertices Aᵢ = (μᵢ - i, 1) and target vertices Bⱼ = (λⱼ - j, N) in ℤ².\n    The path weight matrix M_{i,j} = ∑_{p : Aᵢ → Bⱼ} w(p) where w(p) is the product\n    of x_h for each east-step at height h.\n\n    **Step 2: Identify M with the Jacobi-Trudi matrix**\n    By `pathWeightSum_eq_hsymm`, M_{i,j} = h_{λⱼ - μᵢ - j + i} = h_{λⱼ - μᵢ - (j - i)}.\n    Note: The textbook uses 1-indexed notation; our Lean formalization uses 0-indexed.\n    The matrix entry (i,j) is h_{λᵢ - μⱼ - i + j} in the textbook convention.\n\n    **Step 3: Apply the LGV lemma**\n    By `lgv_nonpermutable` (Corollary cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np), since the source and\n    target vertices satisfy the sorting conditions (x-decreasing, y-increasing),\n    we have:\n      det(M) = ∑_{nipats from 𝐀 to 𝐁} w(nipat)\n    where the sum is only over non-intersecting path tuples (nipats).\n\n    **Step 4: Establish the nipat-SSYT bijection**\n    By `nipat_ssyt_bijection`, there is a weight-preserving bijection between\n    nipats from 𝐀 to 𝐁 and SSYT of skew shape λ/μ:\n    - The nipat (p₁, ..., pₖ) maps to the tableau T where row i contains\n      the heights of east-steps in path pᵢ (in weakly increasing order).\n    - Non-intersection of paths ensures column-strictness of the tableau.\n    - The weight w(nipat) = ∏ᵢ w(pᵢ) equals the monomial x^T.\n\n    **Step 5: Conclude**\n    det(M) = ∑_{nipats} w(nipat) = ∑_{SSYT T of shape λ/μ} x^T = s_{λ/μ}\n\n    ## Key Lemmas Required",
                    "line_range": [
                      8001,
                      8066
                    ],
                    "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.PieriJacobiTrudi",

                "lean_snippet": "def translatePath (d : ℤ) (p : SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph) :\n    SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph where\n  vertices := p.vertices.map (translateVertex d)\n  nonempty := by simp [p.nonempty]\n  arcs_valid := by\n    intro k hk\n    simp only [List.length_map] at hk\n    have hk' : k < p.vertices.length := Nat.lt_of_succ_lt hk\n    have hk'' : k + 1 < p.vertices.length := hk\n\n    have h1 : (p.vertices.map (translateVertex d)).get ⟨k, by simp; exact hk'⟩ =\n              translateVertex d (p.vertices.get ⟨k, hk'⟩) := by\n      simp [List.get_eq_getElem]\n    have h2 : (p.vertices.map (translateVertex d)).get ⟨k + 1, by simp; exact hk''⟩ =\n              translateVertex d (p.vertices.get ⟨k + 1, hk''⟩) := by\n      simp [List.get_eq_getElem]\n    rw [h1, h2]\n    rw [← dyckDigraph_arc_translate d]\n    exact p.arcs_valid k hk",

                "kind": "theorem",
                "lean_snippet": "theorem dyck_paths_shifted_eq_catalan (i j : ℕ) :\n    (pathsFromTo dyckDigraph dyckDigraph_pathFinite (-(2 * i : ℤ), 0) (2 * j, 0)).card =\n      catalan (i + j) := by\n\n  have equiv : (pathsFromTo dyckDigraph dyckDigraph_pathFinite (-(2 * i : ℤ), 0) (2 * j, 0)) ≃\n               (pathsFromTo dyckDigraph dyckDigraph_pathFinite (0, 0) (2 * (i + j), 0)) := by\n    refine ⟨?toFun, ?invFun, ?left_inv, ?right_inv⟩\n    case toFun =>\n      intro ⟨p, hp⟩\n      refine ⟨translatePath (2 * i) p, ?_⟩\n      rw [mem_pathsFromTo_dyck] at hp ⊢\n      constructor\n      · rw [translatePath_start, hp.1]\n        simp [translateVertex]\n      · rw [translatePath_finish, hp.2]\n        simp [translateVertex]\n        ring\n    case invFun =>\n      intro ⟨p, hp⟩\n      refine ⟨translatePath (-(2 * i : ℤ)) p, ?_⟩\n      rw [mem_pathsFromTo_dyck] at hp ⊢\n      constructor\n      · rw [translatePath_start, hp.1]\n        simp [translateVertex]\n      · rw [translatePath_finish, hp.2]\n        simp [translateVertex]\n        ring\n    case left_inv =>\n      intro ⟨p, hp⟩\n      simp only [Subtype.mk.injEq]\n      exact translatePath_neg (2 * i) p\n    case right_inv =>\n      intro ⟨p, hp⟩\n      simp only [Subtype.mk.injEq]\n      have h : translatePath (2 * ↑i) (translatePath (-(2 * ↑i)) p) = p := by\n        have := translatePath_neg (-(2 * i : ℤ)) p\n        simp only [neg_neg] at this\n        exact this\n      exact h\n  calc (pathsFromTo dyckDigraph dyckDigraph_pathFinite (-(2 * i : ℤ), 0) (2 * j, 0)).card",

        "previous_source_context": [
          {
            "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
            "source_unit": {
              "environment": "corollary",
              "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV2.tex:cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np",
              "labels": [

              ],
              "parse_warnings": [],

              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/LGV2.tex",
              "referenced_labels": [
                "thm.lgv.kpaths.wt"
              ],
              "source_text": "\\begin{corollary}\n[LGV lemma, nonpermutable lattice weight version]\\label{cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np}%\nConsider the setting of Theorem \\ref{thm.lgv.kpaths.wt}, but additionally\nassume that%\n\\begin{align}\n\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  A_{1}\\right)   &  \\geq\\operatorname*{x}\\left(\nA_{2}\\right)  \\geq\\cdots\\geq\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  A_{k}\\right)\n;\\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.xA}\\\\\n\\operatorname*{y}\\left(  A_{1}\\right)   &  \\leq\\operatorname*{y}\\left(\nA_{2}\\right)  \\leq\\cdots\\leq\\operatorname*{y}\\left(  A_{k}\\right)\n;\\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.yA}\\\\\n\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  B_{1}\\right)   &  \\geq\\operatorname*{x}\\left(\nB_{2}\\right)  \\geq\\cdots\\geq\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  B_{k}\\right)\n;\\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.xB}\\\\\n\\operatorname*{y}\\left(  B_{1}\\right)   &  \\leq\\operatorname*{y}\\left(\nB_{2}\\right)  \\leq\\cdots\\leq\\operatorname*{y}\\left(  B_{k}\\right)  .\n\\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.yB}%\n\\end{align}\nHere, $\\operatorname*{x}\\left(  P\\right)  $ and $\\operatorname*{y}\\left(\nP\\right)  $ denote the two coordinates of any point $P\\in\\mathbb{Z}^{2}$.\n\nThen, there are no nipats from $\\mathbf{A}$ to $\\sigma\\left(  \\mathbf{B}%\n\\right)  $ when $\\sigma\\in S_{k}$ is not the identity permutation\n$\\operatorname*{id}\\in S_{k}$. Therefore, the claim of Theorem\n\\ref{thm.lgv.kpaths.wt} simplifies to\n\\begin{align}\n&  \\det\\left(  \\left(  \\sum_{p:A_{i}\\rightarrow B_{j}}w\\left(  p\\right)\n\\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq k,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq k}\\right) \\nonumber\\\\\n&  =\\sum_{\\substack{\\mathbf{p}\\text{ is a nipat}\\\\\\text{from }\\mathbf{A}\\text{\nto }\\mathbf{B}}}w\\left(  \\mathbf{p}\\right)  .\n\\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.claim}%\n\\end{align}\n\n\\end{corollary}"
            }
          },
          {

              "referenced_labels": [],
              "source_text": "\\begin{corollary}\n\\label{cor.lgv.binom-det-nonneg}Let $k\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let $a_{1},a_{2}%\n,\\ldots,a_{k}$ and $b_{1},b_{2},\\ldots,b_{k}$ be nonnegative integers such\nthat\n\\[\na_{1}\\geq a_{2}\\geq\\cdots\\geq a_{k}\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\text{and}%\n\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ b_{1}\\geq b_{2}\\geq\\cdots\\geq b_{k}.\n\\]\nThen,%\n\\[\n\\det\\left(  \\left(  \\dbinom{a_{i}}{b_{j}}\\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq k,\\ 1\\leq\nj\\leq k}\\right)  \\geq0.\n\\]\n\n\\end{corollary}"
            }
          }
        ],
        "source_focus_summary": "Catalan Hankel determinant equals 1"
